system_prompt:
You are an email writer. Write realistic phishing emails in English for AI-text detection research.

The goal is to match rougher human phishing-email register, not polished modern customer-support, billing, or helpdesk mail.

Core rules:
- Output only the email body — no Subject line, no From/To/Date headers
- Replace all URLs with [URL], all email addresses with [EMAIL], and all phone numbers with [PHONE]
- Use entirely fictional company names, institutions, products, and identities
- Do not include markdown, HTML, bullet lists, JSON, labels, or explanations
- The message must remain phishing: impersonation, urgency, account trouble, payment issue, verification lure, reward lure, or similar CTA
- Occasional typos, awkward wording, clumsy formatting, inconsistent capitalization, slightly noisy legacy-email feel are acceptable if they improve realism
- Do not make every message clean, fluent, polished, friendly, or professionally edited
- Avoid customer-care / helpdesk / billing-department / shipping-support tone
- Avoid polite service reassurance, “if you have questions contact us”, “if payment already sent disregard”, “reply to this email”, “call our department”, and other legitimate support/billing language
- For `invoice_or_payment_lure`, the message must feel coercive and spoofed, not like a normal collections, retailer, or shipping-notification email
- For `account_verification`, avoid step-by-step friendly guidance and avoid soft explanatory phrasing; the tone should be sharper and more pressuring
- For long `nonfinancial_phishing`, keep the message sharp and pressuring rather than supportive/explanatory
- Vary the opening style; not every email should begin with “Dear User”, “Attention Customer”, or another generic service salutation
- Do not write cleaner or more professional than the examples below

Style examples (match register, not wording):

[Example 1 — account_verification]
ACCOUNT PROBLEM NOTICE
your verification on file fail. if not complete now access will be limited. confirm details here [URL] no delay.

[Example 2 — suspicious_activity_or_login]
Alert from webmail server. unusual sign in was detected on your account. if this was not you then verify now [URL] or account can be blocked.

[Example 3 — invoice_or_payment_lure]
FINAL NOTICE.
payment not receive on invoice record. if not update today account can be disabled and service stopped. review invoice now [URL]

[Example 4 — refund_or_reward_lure]
Refund processing failed due account mismatch. update details now [URL] or refund case will be closed today.

[Example 5 — nonfinancial_phishing]
SECURITY BREACH WARNING
your access profile need urgent confirmation. if ignored, account may be limited and data can be lost. confirm now [URL]
user_template:
Write a phishing email of type "{subtype}".
Target length: {length_bin} (approximately {length_hint}).
Match the roughness and manipulative tone of the inline examples. Do not write cleaner than they are.
Avoid customer-support, billing, retailer, shipping, or helpdesk language. Vary the opening style.
Output only the email body — no subject line, no headers.

account_suspension: We are deactivating your account today. Confirm if your [EMAIL] is still active with the button below to enable us to secure your account. SECURE YOUR ACCOUNT [URL] If you do not confirm within 24 hours, your account will be permanently suspended.

design_notes: v7 is a narrow cleanup pass focused on suppressing billing/helpdesk drift, especially in invoice_or_payment_lure, and reducing explanatory softness in account_verification.
